data.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G3 (child coverage) of parent epic `5fd2` × child `ca03` (Criteria registry + orchestrator). Net: this child is the natural owner of the parent's "criteria registry + orchestrator + chunking/routing" slice and covers MOST of it cleanly — but ONE sub-clause of the parent's routing/budget AC (per-plan BUDGET cap + chunk-atomic checkpointing + cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding) is UNCOVERED by this child AND by every sibling. One uncovered-criterion finding below, then the affirmed coverage map.

═══ FINDING — Parent routing/budget AC's "per-plan BUDGET cap + chunk-atomic checkpointing + cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first)" is UNCOVERED by ca03 and unowned across the whole roster ═══
- criteria: [G3]
- location: child `ca03` "What"/Acceptance-Criteria (orchestrator: applies_at routing, facet chunker, exec-tier dispatch, overlay routing, size-handling ladder, container per-child, Pass-4 dispatch, finding-id mint) — NO budget-cap/checkpoint/shedding clause. Parent `5fd2` AC: "Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by size+complexity+centrality ...; **per-plan budget cap with chunk-atomic checkpointing and an INDETERMINATE outcome** (non-blocking default, configurable)." Also the parent Tests/verification AC: "**cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first)**" is named as a required proving check. Parent design summary: "Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing; INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first."
- evidence:
  - ca03's routing/orchestration ACs cover: applies_at filtering, facet packing, exec-tier dispatch, and a SIZE-HANDLING ladder (batch → one-criterion/call → escalate-model → P8 too-big failure finding). That ladder addresses CONTEXT-WINDOW overflow ("too big to FIT"), which is a DISTINCT concern from the parent's COST/BUDGET cap ($0.15 + n_agentic×$0.25 + n_children×$0.25 per the Experiment-grounded-defaults section).
  - Code grounded: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:561-595` — the only cap implemented is the ADVISORY SURFACING cap (top-N=10 by priority score, owned by `487d`/`55de`), with blocking EXEMPT. INDETERMINATE arises ONLY from "no verification" (`passes.py:454`) / the `indeterminate and not surfaced` branch (`orchestrator.py:587`) — NOT from a budget cap-hit. A `search_files` for `budget`, `checkpoint`, `shed` across `src/` returns NO per-plan budget cap, NO chunk-atomic checkpointing, and NO cap-hit shedding logic in the plan-review pipeline.
  - Roster cross-check (the absence is real, not deferred to a sibling): `012e` (DET floor), `1913`/`acc1`/`487d` (passes 1/2/3), `4bb7` (attestation), `092b` (claim), `db7b` (sidecar), `75a9` (coach), `b711` (overlays), `681b`/`7284` (ISF/eval), `6c5c` (docs), `ce75` (kill-switch/rollout). NONE carries a per-plan budget-cap / checkpoint / cap-hit-shedding AC. `487d`'s cap is the priority-score surfacing cap; `db7b` only RECORDS per-pass latency + an LLM-call cost PROXY (it does not enforce a budget). So no sibling owns this clause.
  - The gate's OWN dogfood run already flagged this exact orphan: `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/final_gate_cycle3.json:920` / `.log:42-43` — "The per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing, and cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first) are a named parent acceptance criterion but are not assigned to any child," with evidence "Child ca03 What ... no budget/checkpoint/INDETERMINATE" and the failure mode "A heavy-epic review exceeds the $0.15 + n×$0.25 cap mid-run; with no child having built checkpointing/shedding, the run fails hard instead of returning INDETERMINATE." This corroborates the absence from an independent run.
- scenarios:
  - A heavy epic with many AGENT-tier / container (G3/G4 fan-out per child) criteria runs past the per-plan cost cap mid-review. With no budget cap + no chunk-atomic checkpointing + no agent/overlay-first shedding, the orchestrator has no defined cap-hit path — it either runs to completion ignoring the configured cap (the cost cap is unenforced) or fails hard, rather than returning the parent-specified non-blocking INDETERMINATE-with-shedding.
  - A project sets a budget cap via config expecting it to bound cost (parent AC: "Per-plan budget cap ... configurable"); there is no code that reads/enforces it, so the configuration silently no-ops.
- impact: Partial coverage of a named parent AC. The cost-control + graceful-degradation half of "proportionate-depth routing + per-plan budget cap + INDETERMINATE shedding" has no owner; the parent's Tests/verification AC ("cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (agent/overlay-first)") therefore has no implementation to prove against. (Note: this is calibration-INDEPENDENT — budget-cap enforcement is structural, not threshold tuning, so it is NOT covered by the parent's "calibration is post-implementation" carve-out.)
- checklist_item:
  - [ ] Assign the parent's "per-plan BUDGET cap (cost: $0.15 + n_agentic×$0.25 + n_children×$0.25) + chunk-atomic checkpointing + cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding (sheds lowest-priority AGENT/overlay criteria first, never the cheap DET/single-turn base; non-blocking default, configurable)" to an owner — either add it as an explicit AC on `ca03` (the orchestrator is the natural home, distinct from its CONTEXT-window size ladder) or file a dedicated child — so the parent AC + its proving check are covered.
- suggested_fix: Add to `ca03` (or a new sibling) an AC: "Per-plan budget cap (config-driven, default = $0.15 base + n_agentic_fired×$0.25 + container-fan-out n_children×$0.25); chunk-atomic checkpointing so a cap-hit between chunks is recoverable; on cap-hit emit a non-blocking INDETERMINATE that SHEDS the lowest-priority AGENT/overlay criteria first (never the DET/single-turn base), with a unit test for the cap-hit shedding order." Disambiguate it from ca03's existing CONTEXT-WINDOW size ladder (which solves fit, not cost).

═══ AFFIRMED COVERAGE MAP (4-bucket; parent criteria this child is the natural owner of) ═══
- Parent AC "Criteria registry (declarative descriptors) + generic orchestrator, reusing rebar.llm extension points" → FULLY covered (ca03 AC1: registry loads from the prompt library + coverage guard; AC2: applies_at filter + facet packing + exec-tier dispatch; built on the shipped e6d9 workflow engine + da27 prompt model).
- Parent AC "Configurable chunking; proportionate-depth routing (complexity + has_children); escalation by size+complexity+centrality" → FULLY covered (ca03 facet chunker base_chunk×size_factor + applies_at proportionate scrutiny + context-window escalation ladder). The "per-plan budget cap + chunk-atomic checkpointing + INDETERMINATE shedding" SUB-CLAUSE of this same AC → UNCOVERED (the Finding above) — so this parent AC is PARTIALLY covered.
- Parent T8 DET-execution-path clarification ("exec=DET dispatched via a code executor alongside the LLM tiers") → FULLY covered (ca03's added T8 AC; `run_det_floor` dispatched before the LLM tiers — invokes 012e's DET floor).
- Parent "NO TRUNCATION / never chunk ticket content; container parent+child ONE-CHILD-AT-A-TIME; absence verified against the COMPLETE set; too-big pairing is a failure finding" → FULLY covered (ca03 AC4/AC5 + `_run_container` per-child loop; remediated and verify-PASS).
- Parent finding-id ownership ("stable `id` minted by the orchestrator, SOLE owner; coach + sidecar CONSUME") → FULLY covered (ca03 mints via `mint_finding_id`; `75a9`/`db7b` consume — cross-checked, non-circular).
- Parent Pass-4 dispatch ("after Pass-3 the orchestrator dispatches the coach") → FULLY covered (ca03 dispatches `75a9`; the coach criterion-implementation itself is `75a9`'s slice, correctly NOT duplicated here).
- COVERED-BY-A-NAMED-SIBLING (so NOT an absence against this pairing — roster-verified): the ADVISORY surfacing cap (top-N=10, blocking exempt) → `487d` (Pass-3) + `55de` (config owner); per-pass latency + LLM-call cost PROXY recording → `db7b`; individual criterion implementations (finder/verifier/decider/DET/overlays/ISF/coach) → `1913`/`acc1`/`487d`/`012e`/`b711`/`681b`/`75a9`; attestation/claim/eval/docs/kill-switch → `4bb7`/`092b`/`7284`/`6c5c`/`ce75`.
- STRUCTURAL: ca03 stays in-scope (orchestration spine) and does not over-reach into sibling-owned criterion implementations.

Net: ONE uncovered parent-AC sub-clause (per-plan budget cap + chunk-atomic checkpointing + cap-hit INDETERMINATE shedding) — unowned across the entire roster, corroborated by the gate's own final_gate_cycle3 dogfood finding. Every OTHER parent criterion in ca03's slice is fully covered, and every adjacent criterion it defers is owned by a named sibling.
